name: Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop
name_original: Por el Cañón del Forcadura — Circular en la Comarca de Sanabria
description: Cycle the difficult 22.7-mile Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop, gaining 2907 feet through stunning mountain scenery.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Lake de Sanabria offers routes through a landscape shaped by glacial activity, featuring deep valleys, cirques, and numerous smaller glacial lakes. The terrain ascends from the lake's shores, encompassing rolling hills and extensive oak forests. The Tera River meanders through the park, adding to the scenic beauty and providing diverse environments for cyclists. This region provides a unique backdrop for touring cycling, with varied ecosystems and geological formations. Best touring cycling routes around Lake de Sanabria The most popular touring cycling route is Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop, a 22.8 miles (36.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route features the high-altitude Laguna de los Peces and challenging climbs. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puebla de Sanabria Old Town – Tera River loop from Cobreros, a difficult 23.7 miles (38.1 km) path. This route winds through traditional villages and along the scenic Tera River. Local touring cyclists also love the Chiringuito at Lake Sanabria – Galende loop from Lago de Sanabria, a 13.0 miles (20.9 km) trail leading through lakeside areas, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Lake de Sanabria is defined by glacial valleys, extensive oak forests, and the shores of the largest glacial lake in the Iberian Peninsula. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate lakeside paths to more difficult climbs. The routes in Lake de Sanabria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lake de Sanabria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lake de Sanabria?
A: There are 13 touring cycling routes around Lake de Sanabria. These include 1 easy route, 7 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, offering options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Lake de Sanabria?
A: The terrain around Lake de Sanabria is shaped by its glacial origins, featuring diverse environments from rolling hills to mountainous areas. You can expect routes with significant elevation changes, particularly on trails leading to higher points like the Laguna de los Peces. Some sections may be unpaved and challenging, while others offer more moderate lakeside paths.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Lake de Sanabria?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Lake de Sanabria are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1404123450/laguna-de-los-peces-cima-zm03-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 22.8-mile route with significant climbs. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chiringuito at Lake Sanabria – Galende loop from Lago de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 13.0 miles through lakeside areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes around Lake de Sanabria offer stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can expect views of the largest glacial lake in the Iberian Peninsula, the Tera River, and extensive oak forests. Notable attractions include the charming traditional village of Puebla de Sanabria with its castle, and the historic Visigothic monastery in San Martín de Castañeda, which now serves as a Park Interpretation Centre.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Lake de Sanabria?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. Generally, routes around the lake offer varied difficulty, with some sections being more suitable for families seeking less challenging terrain. It's advisable to check the specific route details for elevation gain and surface type to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Lake de Sanabria?
A: The Lake Sanabria region is suitable for touring cycling during much of the year, but the best time is typically from spring through early autumn. During these months, the weather is generally milder, and the natural park's diverse ecosystems are in full bloom. High-altitude routes might be more accessible outside of winter due to snow or colder conditions.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Lake de Sanabria?
A: The Sanabria Lake Natural Park is known for its rich biodiversity and natural beauty. While many outdoor activities are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to check specific park regulations and route guidelines regarding pets, especially concerning protected wildlife areas. Keep dogs on a leash and ensure they do not disturb local fauna.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access touring cycling routes in Lake de Sanabria?
A: Information on specific public transport options for cyclists directly to trailheads is limited. However, the region does have some public transport services connecting villages. It's best to research local bus schedules or taxi services that might accommodate bicycles if you plan to use public transport to reach your starting point.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes around Lake de Sanabria?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main villages and popular access points to the Lake Sanabria Natural Park. For routes starting near the lake, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chiringuito at Lake Sanabria – Galende loop from Lago de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking near the lakeside areas. For routes originating from villages like Cobreros, parking would be available within the village itself.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the Lake Sanabria area, particularly around the lake shores and in villages like Puebla de Sanabria and San Martín de Castañeda, offers various cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chiringuito at Lake Sanabria – Galende loop from Lago de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; even highlights a 'Chiringuito' (beach bar/restaurant) as part of its name, indicating lakeside dining options.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Lake de Sanabria?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lake de Sanabria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Over 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning glacial landscapes, the scenic beauty of the lake and Tera River, and the diverse environments ranging from oak forests to higher-altitude scrubland.

Q: What kind of accommodation is available for cyclists in the Lake Sanabria area?
A: The Lake Sanabria region offers a range of accommodation options suitable for cyclists, including hotels, guesthouses, and rural cottages in villages like Puebla de Sanabria and around the lake. Some establishments may offer specific amenities for cyclists, such as secure bike storage. It's advisable to check with individual accommodations for cyclist-friendly services.
tips: Nice beach bar on the edge of the lake. It has food service and has toilets. A good place to stop and enjoy the views.
Last town where you can get water, it has a convent and the water from the fountain is cold

The first section of the route to El Puente follows a beautiful path along the banks of the Tera River. And then the traditional trails continue through Trefacio and San Justo while the track climbs to the high towns of Sanabria.
At this point there are wonderful views of the flow of the Tera river before it merges with the Sanabria lake. An extraordinary lake of glacial origin that is the largest in Spain.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. The ascent to Laguna de los Peces is a significant challenge, featuring a sustained climb of 15.9 kilometers with an average gradient of 4.5%, and ramps reaching up to 9%. It demands considerable effort and is best suited for cyclists with good fitness and experience in mountainous terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows the paved ZA-103 road. While paved, the surface can be bumpy, cracked, or uneven due to extreme temperature fluctuations at this altitude. Cyclists should be prepared for irregular gradients and sections that require strong climbing ability.

faqs.question: What scenic views or landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular natural scenery within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park. You'll experience breathtaking vistas of glacier-carved terrain and the expansive Lake Sanabria. Notable highlights include the Monastery of San Martín de Castañeda and various View of the Tera River viewpoints as you gain altitude.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn. Winter cycling is not recommended due to heavy snow and ice, which make the high-altitude sections impassable and dangerous.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, cyclists often start their ascent from villages like San Martín de Castañeda. You'll likely find parking options in or near these villages before beginning the climb on the ZA-103 road.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a touring cycling route on a public road (ZA-103) within a Natural Park, dogs are generally allowed. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and be mindful of other road users and wildlife in the protected area.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in Sanabria Lake Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads like the ZA-103 within the Parque Natural Lago de Sanabria y Sierras Segundera y de Porto. However, always respect park regulations regarding waste, noise, and wildlife protection.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop shares sections with or is in close proximity to other routes. Notably, it covers significant portions of the official [CIMA ZM03] Laguna de los Peces ~ ZA-103 and [CIMA ZM03] Laguna de los Peces ~ Atajo Castañeda routes. You might also encounter parts of the Montaña de Sanabria - Etapa 1 or the Senda del Cañón del Río Forcadura.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find amenities in the villages surrounding Lake Sanabria. For example, there's a Chiringuito at Lake Sanabria. Villages like San Martín de Castañeda or those closer to Lake Sanabria offer various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and high-mountain environment, pack appropriate touring cycling gear, including layers of clothing for varying temperatures, rain gear, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, and a first-aid kit. The road surface can be bumpy, so a well-maintained bicycle with suitable tires is essential.

faqs.question: Is there an easier alternative for touring cyclists who want to experience the area?
faqs.answer: While the full Laguna de los Peces climb is challenging, you could explore less demanding routes around Lake Sanabria itself. These often offer beautiful scenery without the intense elevation gain. However, if your goal is the CIMA ZM03, be prepared for a significant physical effort.
